message.content: нужно продумать какие инструменты создать в клоде, что бы можно было легко выполнять задачи фронта, что бы было надежно и не пришлось тратить много времени на ревью результата ultrathink поищи в интеренете, давай подумаем с чего нужно начать, и поэтапно выполним

content.thinking:
Пользователь хочет создать инструменты для Claude Code, чтобы упростить выполнение фронтенд-задач. Он хочет, чтобы результаты были надежными и не требовали много времени на ревью.

Я в режиме планирования, поэтому мне нужно:
1. Исследовать текущую структуру проекта и существующие инструменты
2. Поискать в интернете лучшие практики для Claude Code инструментов
3. Понять какие задачи чаще всего выполняются на фронте
4. Разработать план создания инструментов

Начну с исследования - запущу Explore агентов для изучения текущей структуры и поищу в интернете информацию о лучших практиках.

content.text: Отличная идея! Давай исследуем текущую структуру проекта, существующие инструменты и лучшие практики для создания надежных Claude Code инструментов.

Web search results for query: "Claude Code custom agents slash commands best practices 2025"

Links: [{"title":"Claude Code: Best practices for agentic coding","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices"},{"title":"Subagents - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/sub-agents"},{"title":"How I use Claude Code (+ my best tips)","url":"https://www.builder.io/blog/claude-code"},{"title":"Cooking with Claude Code: The Complete Guide - Sid Bharath","url":"https://www.siddharthbharath.com/claude-code-the-complete-guide/"},{"title":"ClaudeLog - Claude Code Docs, Guides, Tutorials & Best Practices","url":"https://claudelog.com/claude-code-changelog/"},{"title":"Customize Claude Code with plugins | Claude","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-code-plugins"},{"title":"GitHub - wshobson/commands: A collection of production-ready slash commands for Claude Code","url":"https://github.com/wshobson/commands"},{"title":"Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk"},{"title":"GitHub - hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code: A curated list of awesome commands, files, and workflows for Claude Code","url":"https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code"},{"title":"Claude Code Slash Commands - The Complete Reference Guide - First Principles Consulting Group","url":"https://firstprinciplescg.com/resources/claude-code-slash-commands-the-complete-reference-guide/"}]

Here are the search results for Claude Code custom agents and slash commands best practices in 2025:

## Key Findings

### Custom Slash Commands

Custom slash commands can include the special keyword $ARGUMENTS to pass parameters from command invocation. For example, you can create a slash command to automatically pull and fix a GitHub issue using: "Please analyze and fix the GitHub issue: $ARGUMENTS."(no content)The slash commands you see in the list come as Claude defaults, but you can create your own. Custom slash commands let you encode repeatable processes and workflows that are specific to your team or project. You first setup a folder to store the custom commands as markdown files, then create markdown files in that folder for each custom command.(no content)You can simply ask Claude to create a custom slash command for you. For example, try asking it to create a command called /branch which checks the current git status and creates a new git branch.

### Custom Agents (Subagents)

When creating subagents, include specific instructions, best practices, and any constraints the subagent should follow.(no content)A key recommendation is to start with Claude-generated agents: "We highly recommend generating your initial subagent with Claude and then iterating on it to make it personally yours."(no content)The /agents command opens a comprehensive interface for creating and managing specialized AI subagents. This command allows you to define custom subagents with their own system prompts, tool permissions, and separate context windows. The interface lists all available tools, including any connected MCP server tools.

### Plugins (New in 2025)

Claude Code now supports plugins that include: Slash commands (custom shortcuts for frequently-used operations), Subagents (purpose-built agents for specialized development tasks), MCP servers (connect to tools and data sources), and Hooks. You can install plugins directly within Claude Code using the /plugin command, now in public beta.(no content)Plugins help you standardize Claude Code environments around a set of shared best practices.

### Best Practices

Pro tip: use /clear often. Every time you start something new, clear the chat.(no content)There are collections of production-ready slash commands for Claude Code that provide intelligent automation and multi-agent orchestration capabilities for modern software development.(no content)The "Claude Code PM" project offers a comprehensive and feature-packed project-management workflow for Claude Code with numerous specialized agents, slash-commands, and strong documentation.

### Community Resources

Plugin marketplaces amplify the best practices the community has developed. Engineer Dan Ávila's plugin marketplace offers plugins for DevOps automation, documentation generation, project management, and testing suites, while engineer Seth Hobson has curated over 80 specialized sub-agents in his GitHub repository.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me provide a detailed summary of the Claude Code tools, agents, and commands in this project.

## Comprehensive Summary: Claude Code Tools, Agents, and Commands

### Overview

This project has a sophisticated multi-agent system for collaborative development. The setup is distributed across:
- **Global level**: `/Users/dmitry/.claude/` - User's personal Claude Code configuration
- **Project level**: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/` - Project-specific settings
- **Client package level**: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/` - Client-specific agents and docs

---

## 1. AGENTS (Workflow Roles)

### Project Agents (Client Package)
Located: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/agents/`

#### 1.1 Business Analyst Agent
**File**: `business-analyst.md`
- **Purpose**: Clarifies requirements and defines WHAT needs to be built
- **Model**: Opus (configurable to Sonnet/Haiku)
- **Tools**: Read, Write, TodoWrite, Bash
- **Workflow**: 
  - Phase 1: Research existing business rules, ask 2-4 clarifying questions
  - Phase 2: Write requirements after user answers
  - Max 2 clarification rounds before escalation
- **Output**: `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md`
- **Status Format**: Must start with `Status: Done`, `Status: Failed - reason`, or `Status: Needs Review - explanation`

#### 1.2 Developer Agent
**File**: `developer.md`
- **Purpose**: Designs technical solutions and writes production-ready code
- **Model**: Opus (configurable)
- **Tools**: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash
- **Key Responsibilities**:
  - Read context and BA requirements
  - Search for similar implementations in codebase
  - Design optimal approach (reuse > create, minimal changes)
  - Implement following project patterns
- **Code Standards**:
  - TypeScript: use `type` not `interface`
  - React: const arrow functions, memoize with useMemo/useCallback
  - Props type named `PropsT`
  - Path aliases: `Components/`, `Services/`, `Shared/`
  - SCSS modules with mobile-first approach
  - No `any`, `console.log`, or forbidden patterns
- **Output**: `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md`

#### 1.3 Tester Agent
**File**: `tester.md`
- **Purpose**: Verifies code quality, writes tests, finds bugs
- **Model**: Opus (configurable)
- **Tools**: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash
- **Key Responsibilities**:
  - Code review for standards compliance
  - Write unit tests (Vitest)
  - E2E tests (Playwright)
  - Verify acceptance criteria met
  - Check TypeScript types, accessibility, error handling
  - Visual verification (no overlaps, all visible)
- **Task-Blocking Issues**: Return to Developer
- **Output**: `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md`
- **Special Note**: For `quickfix` tasks, performs limited testing only

#### 1.4 Documenter Agent
**File**: `documenter.md`
- **Purpose**: Maintains agent-focused documentation after code changes
- **Model**: Opus (configurable)
- **Tools**: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep
- **Key Responsibilities**:
  - Updates `.claude/docs/` files based on code changes
  - Syncs PROJECT_STRUCTURE, PATTERNS, COMPONENTS, SERVICES, BUSINESS_RULES, ARCHITECTURE
  - Ensures documentation stays source of truth for other agents
- **Critical**: Runs AFTER every `task:create` workflow
- **Output**: `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/documenter-output.md`

---

## 2. SLASH COMMANDS

### Project Commands
Located: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/commands/`

#### 2.1 `/task:create` (Full Workflow)
**File**: `task:create.md`
- **Usage**: `/task:create <task-description>`
- **Workflow**: BA → Developer → Tester → Documenter
- **Initialization**:
  - Creates unique task ID: `task-{unix-timestamp-seconds}`
  - Creates folder: `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/`
  - Creates context.json with taskType="full"
- **When to Use**: New features, changes requiring clarification, business logic changes
- **Output**: Multi-file task report with all agent outputs

#### 2.2 `/task:quickfix` (Quick Fix Workflow)
**File**: `task:quickfix.md`
- **Usage**: `/task:quickfix <bug-description>`
- **Workflow**: Developer → Tester (no BA, no Documenter)
- **Task ID Format**: `quickfix-{unix-timestamp-seconds}`
- **When to Use**: Simple bug fixes, minor UI adjustments, typo fixes, small refactoring
- **When NOT to Use**: New features, changes requiring clarification, business logic changes
- **Output**: Developer and Tester outputs only

#### 2.3 `/pr:create` (Pull Request Creation)
**File**: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/commands/pr:create.md`
- **Usage**: `/pr:create`
- **Mode**: Plan mode (review before execution)
- **Responsibilities**:
  - Compare changes with base branch
  - Ask about new branch vs current
  - Stage and commit changes with proper message
  - Create PR in draft mode
- **PR Standards**:
  - Title format: `ZHY-XXX: <description>`
  - Must include JIRA link: `Task: [ZHY-XXX](https://zuratech.atlassian.net/browse/ZHY-XXX)`
  - Emphasize functional changes
  - Avoid marketing language
  - Never include Claude Code attribution

#### 2.4 `/pr:review` (Pull Request Review)
**File**: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/commands/pr:review.md`
- **Usage**: `/pr:review [pr-number-or-url]`
- **Purpose**: Analyze and review GitHub PRs

### Global Commands
Located: `/Users/dmitry/.claude/commands/`

#### 2.5 `/agent:create`
**File**: `agent:create.md`
- **Purpose**: Create new custom agents
- **Workflow**: 
  1. Select scope (global or project)
  2. Gather requirements (purpose, trigger, model)
  3. Check existing agents
  4. Research patterns
  5. Define agent structure
  6. Create file
  7. Validate with agent-lint
- **Model Options**: haiku (fast), sonnet (balanced), opus (complex)

#### 2.6 `/agent:update`
**File**: `agent:update.md`
- **Purpose**: Apply improvements to agents/commands with backup/rollback support
- **Argument Parsing**:
  - No arguments → check recommendations
  - Name only → ask what to change
  - Name + description → direct update
  - `rollback`/`--r` flags for rollback/research
- **Scope Detection**: Auto-detect global vs project, ask if ambiguous

#### 2.7 `/agent:improve`
**File**: `agent:improve.md`
- **Purpose**: Analyze issues and generate improvement recommendations
- **Approach**: Deep reasoning, does NOT apply changes
- **Output**: Recommendations for manual review

#### 2.8 `/agent:lint`
**File**: `agent:lint.md`
- **Purpose**: Validate agents/commands for quality and conflicts
- **Uses**: Web research and deep reasoning

#### 2.9 `/docs`
**File**: `docs.md`
- **Purpose**: Access Claude Code documentation
- **Usage**:
  - `/docs` - List all topics
  - `/docs <topic>` - Read specific documentation
  - `/docs whats new` - Recent changes
- **Implementation**: Calls `~/.claude-code-docs/claude-docs-helper.sh`

---

## 3. DOCUMENTATION STRUCTURE

Located: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/docs/`

All files are **source of truth for agents**. Agents must check these before proposing implementations.

#### 3.1 PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md
- Quick reference index of codebase
- Directory structure overview
- Links to detailed documentation

#### 3.2 PATTERNS.md
- Practical guide on established patterns
- Implementation examples
- Currently documented:
  - Modal pattern
  - (More patterns in full file)

#### 3.3 COMPONENTS.md
- Detailed component API reference
- Props and usage examples
- Available components catalog

#### 3.4 SERVICES.md
- Service API reference
- Methods and usage examples
- Available services catalog

#### 3.5 BUSINESS_RULES.md
- Business logic and validation rules
- Calculations and edge cases
- Scenarios and acceptance criteria

#### 3.6 ARCHITECTURE.md
- Technical decisions and rationale
- Technology choices explanation
- System design decisions

---

## 4. TASK STRUCTURE

### Directory Layout
```
.claude/tasks/{task-id}/
├── context.json                    # Shared context between agents
├── business-analyst-output.md      # Requirements (full workflow only)
├── developer-output.md             # Implementation details
├── tester-output.md                # Tests and bugs found
├── documenter-output.md            # Documentation updates
└── command-output.md               # Final report summary
```

### Context File Format
```json
{
  "taskId": "task-{timestamp}",
  "taskType": "full|quickfix",
  "createdAt": "ISO timestamp",
  "description": "User task description",
  "currentAgent": "agent-name",
  "completedAgents": [],
  "userAnswers": {}
}
```

### Example Task Output
**Existing Task**: `task-1733333127` (Sidebar improvements)
- All 4 agents completed successfully
- BA: 14 acceptance criteria defined
- Developer: Created useSwipeGesture hook, modified Sidebar.tsx and sidebar.scss
- Tester: 14 new tests, all passing, no bugs
- Documenter: Updated relevant documentation

---

## 5. CONFIGURATION FILES

### Project-Level Settings
- **File**: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/settings.json`
- **Permissions**:
  - Allow: WebFetch, WebSearch, Bash (git, yarn test, ls, env, cat), Write (*.local.md)
  - Deny: Read(.env)
- **Environment**: Telemetry disabled (CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=0)
- **Login**: Console method

### Client-Level Hooks
- **File**: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/settings.local.json`
- **PostToolUse Hooks**:
  - After Write/Edit: Auto-format with Prettier
  - After Write/Edit: Auto-stage files with git add

---

## 6. EXISTING WORKFLOWS & AUTOMATION

### Multi-Agent Task Workflow
1. **Initialization**: Slash command creates task folder and context
2. **Sequential Execution**: Each agent reads previous agent's output
3. **Escalation**: Agent outputs status (Done/Failed/Needs Review)
4. **Documentation Sync**: Final agent updates documentation

### Key Features
- Task isolation (separate folders per task)
- Status tracking (mandatory status format)
- Modular agent design (can skip agents for quickfix)
- Documentation as source of truth
- Automatic formatting and git staging (via hooks)
- Backup system for agent changes (`.backups/` and `.improvements/` directories)

---

## 7. GAPS & MISSING FUNCTIONALITY

### Identified Gaps

1. **No Integration Testing Workflow** - Currently only unit and E2E tests documented
2. **No Database Migration Agents** - Prisma migrations are not explicitly covered in agent responsibilities
3. **No Performance Testing Agent** - No dedicated agent for performance verification
4. **No Accessibility Audit Agent** - Accessibility checks are part of Tester, not specialized
5. **No Deployment/Release Agent** - No agent for managing deployments or version management
6. **No API Contract Verification** - No agent for verifying API contracts between client/server
7. **Limited Conflict Resolution** - No agent for resolving merge conflicts in collaborative scenarios
8. **No Code Diff Review Tool** - Agents manually review code, no automated diff summary tool
9. **No Task Dependencies** - No mechanism for sequential task dependencies
10. **No Documentation Auto-Generation** - Documentation updates are manual, not auto-generated from code

---

## 8. KEY FILE PATHS SUMMARY

| Component | Path |
|-----------|------|
| Project CLAUDE.md | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/CLAUDE.md` |
| Client CLAUDE.md | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/CLAUDE.md` |
| Project Settings | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/settings.json` |
| Client Settings | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/settings.local.json` |
| BA Agent | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/agents/business-analyst.md` |
| Developer Agent | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/agents/developer.md` |
| Tester Agent | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/agents/tester.md` |
| Documenter Agent | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/agents/documenter.md` |
| task:create Command | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/commands/task:create.md` |
| task:quickfix Command | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/commands/task:quickfix.md` |
| pr:create Command | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/commands/pr:create.md` |
| Documentation Root | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/docs/` |
| Global Agents | `/Users/dmitry/.claude/agents/` |
| Global Commands | `/Users/dmitry/.claude/commands/` |
| Task Output Root | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/tasks/` |

---

## 9. USAGE PATTERNS

### Starting a New Feature
```bash
/task:create Add feature description here
# BA asks clarifying questions
# You answer questions
# Developer implements
# Tester verifies
# Documenter updates docs
```

### Quick Bug Fix
```bash
/task:quickfix Fix description here
# Developer implements
# Tester verifies
# No BA or Documenter phases
```

### Create Pull Request
```bash
/pr:create
# Review in plan mode
# Confirm branch and commit details
```

### Modify an Agent
```bash
/agent:update developer add new checklist item
# Or use recommendations: /agent:update (shows recommendations)
```

This comprehensive multi-agent system enables structured, role-based development with clear escalation paths, documentation-driven workflows, and automated task management.

  - Ensures documentation stays source of truth for other agents
- **Critical**: Runs AFTER every `task:create` workflow
- **Output**: `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/documenter-output.md`

---

## 2. SLASH COMMANDS

### Project Commands
Located: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/commands/`

#### 2.1 `/task:create` (Full Workflow)
**File**: `task:create.md`
- **Usage**: `/task:create <task-description>`
- **Workflow**: BA → Developer → Tester → Documenter
- **Initialization**:
  - Creates unique task ID: `task-{unix-timestamp-seconds}`
  - Creates folder: `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/`
  - Creates context.json with taskType="full"
- **When to Use**: New features, changes requiring clarification, business logic changes
- **Output**: Multi-file task report with all agent outputs

#### 2.2 `/task:quickfix` (Quick Fix Workflow)
**File**: `task:quickfix.md`
- **Usage**: `/task:quickfix <bug-description>`
- **Workflow**: Developer → Tester (no BA, no Documenter)
- **Task ID Format**: `quickfix-{unix-timestamp-seconds}`
- **When to Use**: Simple bug fixes, minor UI adjustments, typo fixes, small refactoring
- **When NOT to Use**: New features, changes requiring clarification, business logic changes
- **Output**: Developer and Tester outputs only

#### 2.3 `/pr:create` (Pull Request Creation)
**File**: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/commands/pr:create.md`
- **Usage**: `/pr:create`
- **Mode**: Plan mode (review before execution)
- **Responsibilities**:
  - Compare changes with base branch
  - Ask about new branch vs current
  - Stage and commit changes with proper message
  - Create PR in draft mode
- **PR Standards**:
  - Title format: `ZHY-XXX: <description>`
  - Must include JIRA link: `Task: [ZHY-XXX](https://zuratech.atlassian.net/browse/ZHY-XXX)`
  - Emphasize functional changes
  - Avoid marketing language
  - Never include Claude Code attribution

#### 2.4 `/pr:review` (Pull Request Review)
**File**: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/commands/pr:review.md`
- **Usage**: `/pr:review [pr-number-or-url]`
- **Purpose**: Analyze and review GitHub PRs

### Global Commands
Located: `/Users/dmitry/.claude/commands/`

#### 2.5 `/agent:create`
**File**: `agent:create.md`
- **Purpose**: Create new custom agents
- **Workflow**: 
  1. Select scope (global or project)
  2. Gather requirements (purpose, trigger, model)
  3. Check existing agents
  4. Research patterns
  5. Define agent structure
  6. Create file
  7. Validate with agent-lint
- **Model Options**: haiku (fast), sonnet (balanced), opus (complex)

#### 2.6 `/agent:update`
**File**: `agent:update.md`
- **Purpose**: Apply improvements to agents/commands with backup/rollback support
- **Argument Parsing**:
  - No arguments → check recommendations
  - Name only → ask what to change
  - Name + description → direct update
  - `rollback`/`--r` flags for rollback/research
- **Scope Detection**: Auto-detect global vs project, ask if ambiguous

#### 2.7 `/agent:improve`
**File**: `agent:improve.md`
- **Purpose**: Analyze issues and generate improvement recommendations
- **Approach**: Deep reasoning, does NOT apply changes
- **Output**: Recommendations for manual review

#### 2.8 `/agent:lint`
**File**: `agent:lint.md`
- **Purpose**: Validate agents/commands for quality and conflicts
- **Uses**: Web research and deep reasoning

#### 2.9 `/docs`
**File**: `docs.md`
- **Purpose**: Access Claude Code documentation
- **Usage**:
  - `/docs` - List all topics
  - `/docs <topic>` - Read specific documentation
  - `/docs whats new` - Recent changes
- **Implementation**: Calls `~/.claude-code-docs/claude-docs-helper.sh`

---

## 3. DOCUMENTATION STRUCTURE

Located: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/docs/`

All files are **source of truth for agents**. Agents must check these before proposing implementations.

#### 3.1 PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md
- Quick reference index of codebase
- Directory structure overview
- Links to detailed documentation

#### 3.2 PATTERNS.md
- Practical guide on established patterns
- Implementation examples
- Currently documented:
  - Modal pattern
  - (More patterns in full file)

#### 3.3 COMPONENTS.md
- Detailed component API reference
- Props and usage examples
- Available components catalog

#### 3.4 SERVICES.md
- Service API reference
- Methods and usage examples
- Available services catalog

#### 3.5 BUSINESS_RULES.md
- Business logic and validation rules
- Calculations and edge cases
- Scenarios and acceptance criteria

#### 3.6 ARCHITECTURE.md
- Technical decisions and rationale
- Technology choices explanation
- System design decisions

---

## 4. TASK STRUCTURE

### Directory Layout
```
.claude/tasks/{task-id}/
├── context.json                    # Shared context between agents
├── business-analyst-output.md      # Requirements (full workflow only)
├── developer-output.md             # Implementation details
├── tester-output.md                # Tests and bugs found
├── documenter-output.md            # Documentation updates
└── command-output.md               # Final report summary
```

### Context File Format
```json
{
  "taskId": "task-{timestamp}",
  "taskType": "full|quickfix",
  "createdAt": "ISO timestamp",
  "description": "User task description",
  "currentAgent": "agent-name",
  "completedAgents": [],
  "userAnswers": {}
}
```

### Example Task Output
**Existing Task**: `task-1733333127` (Sidebar improvements)
- All 4 agents completed successfully
- BA: 14 acceptance criteria defined
- Developer: Created useSwipeGesture hook, modified Sidebar.tsx and sidebar.scss
- Tester: 14 new tests, all passing, no bugs
- Documenter: Updated relevant documentation

---

## 5. CONFIGURATION FILES

### Project-Level Settings
- **File**: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/settings.json`
- **Permissions**:
  - Allow: WebFetch, WebSearch, Bash (git, yarn test, ls, env, cat), Write (*.local.md)
  - Deny: Read(.env)
- **Environment**: Telemetry disabled (CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=0)
- **Login**: Console method

### Client-Level Hooks
- **File**: `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/settings.local.json`
- **PostToolUse Hooks**:
  - After Write/Edit: Auto-format with Prettier
  - After Write/Edit: Auto-stage files with git add

---

## 6. EXISTING WORKFLOWS & AUTOMATION

### Multi-Agent Task Workflow
1. **Initialization**: Slash command creates task folder and context
2. **Sequential Execution**: Each agent reads previous agent's output
3. **Escalation**: Agent outputs status (Done/Failed/Needs Review)
4. **Documentation Sync**: Final agent updates documentation

### Key Features
- Task isolation (separate folders per task)
- Status tracking (mandatory status format)
- Modular agent design (can skip agents for quickfix)
- Documentation as source of truth
- Automatic formatting and git staging (via hooks)
- Backup system for agent changes (`.backups/` and `.improvements/` directories)

---

## 7. GAPS & MISSING FUNCTIONALITY

### Identified Gaps

1. **No Integration Testing Workflow** - Currently only unit and E2E tests documented
2. **No Database Migration Agents** - Prisma migrations are not explicitly covered in agent responsibilities
3. **No Performance Testing Agent** - No dedicated agent for performance verification
4. **No Accessibility Audit Agent** - Accessibility checks are part of Tester, not specialized
5. **No Deployment/Release Agent** - No agent for managing deployments or version management
6. **No API Contract Verification** - No agent for verifying API contracts between client/server
7. **Limited Conflict Resolution** - No agent for resolving merge conflicts in collaborative scenarios
8. **No Code Diff Review Tool** - Agents manually review code, no automated diff summary tool
9. **No Task Dependencies** - No mechanism for sequential task dependencies
10. **No Documentation Auto-Generation** - Documentation updates are manual, not auto-generated from code

---

## 8. KEY FILE PATHS SUMMARY

| Component | Path |
|-----------|------|
| Project CLAUDE.md | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/CLAUDE.md` |
| Client CLAUDE.md | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/CLAUDE.md` |
| Project Settings | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/settings.json` |
| Client Settings | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/settings.local.json` |
| BA Agent | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/agents/business-analyst.md` |
| Developer Agent | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/agents/developer.md` |
| Tester Agent | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/agents/tester.md` |
| Documenter Agent | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/agents/documenter.md` |
| task:create Command | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/commands/task:create.md` |
| task:quickfix Command | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/commands/task:quickfix.md` |
| pr:create Command | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/.claude/commands/pr:create.md` |
| Documentation Root | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/docs/` |
| Global Agents | `/Users/dmitry/.claude/agents/` |
| Global Commands | `/Users/dmitry/.claude/commands/` |
| Task Output Root | `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/tasks/` |

---

## 9. USAGE PATTERNS

### Starting a New Feature
```bash
/task:create Add feature description here
# BA asks clarifying questions
# You answer questions
# Developer implements
# Tester verifies
# Documenter updates docs
```

### Quick Bug Fix
```bash
/task:quickfix Fix description here
# Developer implements
# Tester verifies
# No BA or Documenter phases
```

### Create Pull Request
```bash
/pr:create
# Review in plan mode
# Confirm branch and commit details
```

### Modify an Agent
```bash
/agent:update developer add new checklist item
# Or use recommendations: /agent:update (shows recommendations)
```

This comprehensive multi-agent system enables structured, role-based development with clear escalation paths, documentation-driven workflows, and automated task management.

## Client Frontend Codebase Exploration Summary

### Tech Stack

**Core Framework & Routing:**
- React 19.2.0
- React Router DOM 7.9.6
- Vite 7.2.4 (build tool)
- TypeScript 5.8.3

**State Management & Data Fetching:**
- TanStack React Query 5.90.10 (server state, caching, infinite queries)
- Preact Signals React 3.6.0 (global signals-based state management)
- React Hook Form 7.66.1 (form state management)

**Styling:**
- SCSS 1.86.3 with Sass
- CSS Modules or scoped styling pattern (based on file structure)
- CSS custom properties/variables
- Classnames 2.5.1 for conditional classes

**UI Libraries & Components:**
- React Icons 5.5.0
- React DatePicker 8.9.0
- React Plaid Link 4.1.1 (bank integration)
- DND Kit 6.3.1 + 10.0.0 (drag-and-drop sorting)

**HTTP & API:**
- Axios 1.13.2 (HTTP client with custom interceptors)
- Custom requestClient wrapper with error handling (ValidationError, AuthorizationError)

**Error Tracking & Analytics:**
- Sentry React 10.25.0
- Sentry Tracing 7.120.4
- Web Vitals 4.2.4

**Testing:**
- Vitest 4.0.13 (unit/integration tests)
- Playwright 1.56.1 (e2e tests)
- Testing Library (React 16.3.0, DOM 10.4.1, Jest DOM 6.9.1)
- User Event 14.6.1

---

### Folder Structure Overview

```
/src
├── /app                          # Application shell
│   ├── /errorBoundery           # Global error boundary & error UI
│   ├── /notification            # Toast/notification system
│   ├── /modalsObserver          # Modal state management
│   ├── /routes                  # React Router configuration
│   ├── /sentry                  # Sentry error tracking setup
│   ├── /webVitals               # Performance metrics
│   ├── /styles                  # Global SCSS (variables, helpers, fonts, layers)
│   ├── App.tsx                  # Root app component
│   └── ConfigProviders.tsx      # React Query provider setup

├── /components                   # Reusable UI components
│   ├── /form                     # Form-related components
│   │   ├── /button              # AuButton (custom button)
│   │   ├── /field               # Form field wrapper
│   │   ├── /checkbox            # AuCheckbox
│   │   ├── /radioGroup          # AuRadioGroup
│   │   ├── /dropdown            # AuDropdown
│   │   ├── /select              # AuSelect
│   │   ├── /datePicker          # AuDatePicker
│   │   ├── /formBlock           # Form layout wrapper
│   │   ├── AuForm.tsx           # Main form component (react-hook-form wrapper)
│   │   └── /oldFields           # Legacy form components
│   ├── /layouts                  # Layout wrappers
│   │   ├── MainLayout            # App main layout with menu/sidebar
│   │   ├── BaseLayout            # Basic page wrapper
│   │   └── /menu                 # Navigation menu
│   ├── /sidebar                  # Sidebar panel system (portal-based)
│   ├── /modal                    # Modal dialog system
│   ├── /aiAssistant             # AI chat assistant UI
│   ├── /svg                      # SVG icon components

│   ├── EditableLabel             # In-place text editing

│   ├── PageHeader                # Page title/header

├── /pages                        # Page components (feature pages)
│   ├── /transactions            # Transactions module
│   │   ├── /form                # Transaction form components
│   │   ├── /filter              # Transaction filtering
│   │   ├── /list                # Transaction list
│   │   ├── /sidebars            # Sidebar dialogs for transaction actions
│   │   └── TransactionsPage.tsx
│   ├── /budget                  # Single budget detail view
│   ├── /budgets                 # Budget list/management
│   │   ├── /form, /sidebars

│   ├── /userCatigory            # User category management
│   ├── /profile                 # User profile page
│   ├── /auth, /login, /registration # Auth pages
│   └── /logout

├── /services                     # Business logic & side effects
│   ├── authService              # Auth token management
│   ├── modalService             # Modal registry/control
│   ├── sidebarService           # Sidebar registry/control
│   ├── notificationService      # Toast notifications
│   ├── cookieService            # Cookie management
│   ├── scrollLockService        # Body scroll locking
│   ├── localStorageService      # Local storage abstraction
│   └── /aiAssistant             # AI assistant service

├── /repositories                 # API data layer
│   ├── /transactions            # Transaction API hooks
│   ├── /budgets                 # Budget API hooks
│   ├── /bankAccounts            # Bank account API hooks
│   ├── /category                # Category API hooks
│   └── [each has DTO types]

├── /shared                       # Shared utilities
│   ├── /hooks                    # Custom React hooks
│   │   ├── useAsyncFn           # Async state management
│   │   ├── useCancellablePromise
│   │   ├── useSwipeGesture      # Mobile swipe detection
│   │   ├── useEventListener
│   │   ├── useOutsideClickListener
│   │   ├── useIdsList           # ID tracking for deleted items
│   │   └── /ai, /saveRemovalNotification
│   ├── /store                    # Global store (signals)
│   │   ├── globalStore          # Preact signals state
│   │   └── useGlobalValue       # useSyncExternalStore wrapper
│   ├── /requestClient            # HTTP client setup
│   │   ├── axiosClient          # Axios instance with interceptors
│   │   ├── requestClient        # Request wrapper
│   │   └── queryClient          # React Query config
│   ├── /utils                    # Utility functions
│   │   ├── helpers              # hasValue, throttle, debounce
│   │   ├── currencyHelper       # Currency formatting
│   │   ├── dateTimeHelper       # Date/time utilities
│   │   ├── plural               # Pluralization
│   │   ├── errorReporter        # Error logging
│   │   ├── sentryHelpers        # Sentry integration
│   │   ├── isMobile             # Mobile detection
│   │   ├── /url                 # URL/routing utilities
│   │   └── /json                # JSON utilities
│   ├── /types                    # TypeScript types & enums
│   │   ├── /api                 # API request/response types
│   │   ├── /form                # Form types
│   │   ├── /url                 # URL/routing types
│   │   └── GlobalStoreFieldE    # Store field enum
│   ├── /constants               # Constant values
│   ├── /dtos                    # Shared data transfer objects
│   └── /errors                  # Custom error classes

├── /assets                       # Static files
└── index.tsx                     # React entry point
```

---

### Key Patterns & Conventions

**Component Patterns:**
- Functional components with TypeScript
- Props type definition as `PropsT` (convention)
- Default exports for page components
- Named exports for reusable components
- BEM-like CSS class naming: `au-button au-button--primary`
- Classnames utility for conditional CSS

**Form Handling:**
- React Hook Form wrapper (`AuForm`) with error handling
- Validation error mapping from server to form fields
- Custom field components (`AuField`, `AuCheckbox`, etc.)
- Auto-focus first error field on validation failure
- onSubmit error handling with ValidationError parsing

**State Management:**
- Global state via Preact signals + `useSyncExternalStore`
- Server state via React Query (infinite queries, mutations)
- Local component state via useState/useReducer
- Services as singletons for side effects (auth, modals, notifications)

**Data Fetching:**
- Repository layer with custom React Query hooks
- DTO types for API contracts (DTOs with 'S' suffix for send/request)
- Axios client with request/response interceptors
- Auth token in Authorization header
- Error handling: ValidationError (400), AuthorizationError (401)

**Testing:**
- Vitest with jsdom environment
- React Testing Library for component testing
- Unit tests for utilities with describe/it/expect
- Hook testing with renderHook from testing library
- Test file colocation with `src/**/*.test.ts(x)` pattern
- Mocking via vi.fn(), vi.mock()
- Timer mocking for debounce/throttle tests

**Type System:**
- Strict TypeScript (strict: true)
- Enums with 'E' suffix (e.g., `GlobalStoreFieldE`, `AuButtonKindE`)
- Types with 'T' suffix (e.g., `PropsT`, `ModalT`)
- DTOs with 'DTO' suffix
- Service types with explicit typing

**Styling Approach:**
- SCSS with nesting and variables
- Global variables in `/app/styles/variables.scss`
- CSS layers for specificity management
- BEM methodology for component classes
- Classnames utility for conditional classes
- Scoped styles per component

**Service Architecture:**
- Singleton services for state (authService, sidebarService, modalService)
- Services expose simple interfaces (methods on object export)
- useGlobalValue hook for reactive global state
- Portal-based sidebars and modals
- Event-based system (sidebarService.close, sidebarService.finalizeClose)

**Error Handling:**
- Custom error classes: ValidationError, AuthorizationError
- Sentry integration with custom user context from JWT
- Error boundaries at app and route levels
- Form-level validation error mapping
- Error reporter utility for logging

**Utilities & Helpers:**
- Throttle/debounce implementations with release/cancel methods
- URL builders for navigation (appPaths constants)
- Custom hooks for common patterns (useEventListener, useOutsideClickListener, etc.)
- JSON utilities for safe parsing/stringifying
- Mobile detection utility

---

### Testing Coverage Areas

Tested modules include:
- Utility functions (helpers, date/time, currency, URL builders)
- Custom hooks (useAsyncFn, useSwipeGesture, useGlobalValue)
- Services (auth, modal, sidebar, localStorage, notification, cookie)
- API service (aiAssistantService)
- Error handling classes

---

### Areas That Could Benefit from Automation/Tooling

1. **Component Library Scaffold**: Automated generator for new components with boilerplate (component, styles, test, types)

2. **API Hook Generator**: Generate custom React Query hooks from API schema/types to reduce repetitive repository code

3. **Form Code Generation**: Generate form components and types from API request/response DTOs

4. **Test Coverage Dashboard**: Integration to track test coverage per package since tests are split across the codebase

5. **Type Safety Improvements**:
   - Stricter path types for React Router (currently string-based appPaths)
   - API client type safety (could use tRPC or similar for end-to-end typing)

6. **Storybook Integration**: Component documentation and visual testing with Storybook

7. **CSS Architecture Tools**:
   - CSS variable documentation/generation from SCSS
   - Style linting (stylelint) to enforce conventions

8. **Code Generation from Designs**: Tools to auto-generate components from Figma/design files

9. **E2E Test Automation**: Better Playwright integration with visual regression testing

10. **Performance Monitoring**: Automated bundle size tracking and lighthouse CI for performance regression detection

11. **Component Accessibility Audit**: Automated a11y testing across components

12. **Documentation Generator**: Auto-generate component prop docs from TypeScript types

```
│   │   ├── /list                # Transaction list

│   │   └── TransactionsPage.tsx
│   ├── /budget                  # Single budget detail view
│   ├── /budgets                 # Budget list/management
│   │   ├── /form, /sidebars
│   │   └── BudgetsPage.tsx
│   ├── /bankAccounts            # Bank account management
│   ├── /userCatigory            # User category management
│   ├── /profile                 # User profile page
│   ├── /auth, /login, /registration # Auth pages
│   └── /logout

├── /services                     # Business logic & side effects
│   ├── authService              # Auth token management
│   ├── modalService             # Modal registry/control
│   ├── sidebarService           # Sidebar registry/control
│   ├── notificationService      # Toast notifications
│   ├── cookieService            # Cookie management
│   ├── scrollLockService        # Body scroll locking
│   ├── localStorageService      # Local storage abstraction
│   └── /aiAssistant             # AI assistant service

├── /repositories                 # API data layer
│   ├── /transactions            # Transaction API hooks
│   ├── /budgets                 # Budget API hooks
│   ├── /bankAccounts            # Bank account API hooks
│   ├── /category                # Category API hooks
│   └── [each has DTO types]

├── /shared                       # Shared utilities
│   ├── /hooks                    # Custom React hooks
│   │   ├── useAsyncFn           # Async state management
│   │   ├── useCancellablePromise
│   │   ├── useSwipeGesture      # Mobile swipe detection
│   │   ├── useEventListener
│   │   ├── useOutsideClickListener
│   │   ├── useIdsList           # ID tracking for deleted items
│   │   └── /ai, /saveRemovalNotification
│   ├── /store                    # Global store (signals)
│   │   ├── globalStore          # Preact signals state
│   │   └── useGlobalValue       # useSyncExternalStore wrapper
│   ├── /requestClient            # HTTP client setup
│   │   ├── axiosClient          # Axios instance with interceptors
│   │   ├── requestClient        # Request wrapper
│   │   └── queryClient          # React Query config
│   ├── /utils                    # Utility functions
│   │   ├── helpers              # hasValue, throttle, debounce
│   │   ├── currencyHelper       # Currency formatting
│   │   ├── dateTimeHelper       # Date/time utilities
│   │   ├── plural               # Pluralization
│   │   ├── errorReporter        # Error logging
│   │   ├── sentryHelpers        # Sentry integration
│   │   ├── isMobile             # Mobile detection
│   │   ├── /url                 # URL/routing utilities
│   │   └── /json                # JSON utilities
│   ├── /types                    # TypeScript types & enums
│   │   ├── /api                 # API request/response types
│   │   ├── /form                # Form types
│   │   ├── /url                 # URL/routing types
│   │   └── GlobalStoreFieldE    # Store field enum
│   ├── /constants               # Constant values
│   ├── /dtos                    # Shared data transfer objects
│   └── /errors                  # Custom error classes

├── /assets                       # Static files
└── index.tsx                     # React entry point
```

---

### Key Patterns & Conventions

**Component Patterns:**
- Functional components with TypeScript
- Props type definition as `PropsT` (convention)
- Default exports for page components
- Named exports for reusable components
- BEM-like CSS class naming: `au-button au-button--primary`
- Classnames utility for conditional CSS

**Form Handling:**
- React Hook Form wrapper (`AuForm`) with error handling
- Validation error mapping from server to form fields
- Custom field components (`AuField`, `AuCheckbox`, etc.)
- Auto-focus first error field on validation failure
- onSubmit error handling with ValidationError parsing

**State Management:**
- Global state via Preact signals + `useSyncExternalStore`
- Server state via React Query (infinite queries, mutations)
- Local component state via useState/useReducer
- Services as singletons for side effects (auth, modals, notifications)

**Data Fetching:**
- Repository layer with custom React Query hooks
- DTO types for API contracts (DTOs with 'S' suffix for send/request)
- Axios client with request/response interceptors
- Auth token in Authorization header
- Error handling: ValidationError (400), AuthorizationError (401)

**Testing:**
- Vitest with jsdom environment
- React Testing Library for component testing
- Unit tests for utilities with describe/it/expect
- Hook testing with renderHook from testing library
- Test file colocation with `src/**/*.test.ts(x)` pattern
- Mocking via vi.fn(), vi.mock()
- Timer mocking for debounce/throttle tests

**Type System:**
- Strict TypeScript (strict: true)
- Enums with 'E' suffix (e.g., `GlobalStoreFieldE`, `AuButtonKindE`)
- Types with 'T' suffix (e.g., `PropsT`, `ModalT`)
- DTOs with 'DTO' suffix
- Service types with explicit typing

**Styling Approach:**
- SCSS with nesting and variables
- Global variables in `/app/styles/variables.scss`
- CSS layers for specificity management
- BEM methodology for component classes
- Classnames utility for conditional classes
- Scoped styles per component

**Service Architecture:**
- Singleton services for state (authService, sidebarService, modalService)
- Services expose simple interfaces (methods on object export)
- useGlobalValue hook for reactive global state
- Portal-based sidebars and modals
- Event-based system (sidebarService.close, sidebarService.finalizeClose)

**Error Handling:**
- Custom error classes: ValidationError, AuthorizationError
- Sentry integration with custom user context from JWT
- Error boundaries at app and route levels
- Form-level validation error mapping
- Error reporter utility for logging

**Utilities & Helpers:**
- Throttle/debounce implementations with release/cancel methods
- URL builders for navigation (appPaths constants)
- Custom hooks for common patterns (useEventListener, useOutsideClickListener, etc.)
- JSON utilities for safe parsing/stringifying
- Mobile detection utility

---

### Testing Coverage Areas

Tested modules include:
- Utility functions (helpers, date/time, currency, URL builders)
- Custom hooks (useAsyncFn, useSwipeGesture, useGlobalValue)
- Services (auth, modal, sidebar, localStorage, notification, cookie)
- API service (aiAssistantService)
- Error handling classes

---

### Areas That Could Benefit from Automation/Tooling

1. **Component Library Scaffold**: Automated generator for new components with boilerplate (component, styles, test, types)

2. **API Hook Generator**: Generate custom React Query hooks from API schema/types to reduce repetitive repository code

3. **Form Code Generation**: Generate form components and types from API request/response DTOs

4. **Test Coverage Dashboard**: Integration to track test coverage per package since tests are split across the codebase

5. **Type Safety Improvements**:
   - Stricter path types for React Router (currently string-based appPaths)
   - API client type safety (could use tRPC or similar for end-to-end typing)

6. **Storybook Integration**: Component documentation and visual testing with Storybook

7. **CSS Architecture Tools**:
   - CSS variable documentation/generation from SCSS
   - Style linting (stylelint) to enforce conventions

8. **Code Generation from Designs**: Tools to auto-generate components from Figma/design files

9. **E2E Test Automation**: Better Playwright integration with visual regression testing

10. **Performance Monitoring**: Automated bundle size tracking and lighthouse CI for performance regression detection

11. **Component Accessibility Audit**: Automated a11y testing across components

12. **Documentation Generator**: Auto-generate component prop docs from TypeScript types

content.content:
# Claude Code: Custom Agents, Slash Commands & Workflows

## Structuring Agents for Reliability

**Custom Slash Commands**: Store prompt templates in Markdown files within `.claude/commands` folder. These become available through the slash commands menu when typing `/`. 

The documentation demonstrates this with a GitHub issue-fixing command that includes structured steps: "Use `gh issue view` to get the issue details," understand the problem, search the codebase, implement changes, write tests, verify linting, create commit messages, and push PRs.

For reliability, slash commands support the `$ARGUMENTS` keyword to pass parameters. This pattern creates reusable, versioned workflows that teams can check into git for consistency across developers.

## Reducing Review Time

**Multi-Claude Verification Pattern**: Deploy separate Claude instances with distinct responsibilities—one writes code while another reviews it independently. This separation often yields better results than single-instance processing.

The guide emphasizes: "This separation often yields better results than having a single Claude handle everything." Additionally, use git worktrees to run parallel sessions on independent tasks without merge conflicts.

**Test-Driven Approaches**: Provide clear targets (tests, visual mocks, screenshots) to minimize iteration cycles. Claude performs best with concrete outputs to evaluate against.

## Multi-Agent Orchestration Patterns

**Subagent Integration**: Within exploration phases, "telling Claude to use subagents to verify details or investigate particular questions" preserves context availability without efficiency loss.

**Headless Mode Fanning**: For large migrations or analyses, have Claude generate task lists, then loop through programmatically, calling Claude with specific tasks and permitted tools per iteration.

**Pipelining**: Integrate Claude into data pipelines using `claude -p "<prompt>" --output-format stream-json | your_command` for structured downstream processing.

## Error Handling & Escalation

**Context Management**: Use `/clear` frequently between tasks to reset context windows and refocus performance during long sessions.

**Course Correction Tools**:
- Ask Claude to plan before coding
- Press Escape to interrupt and preserve context
- Double-tap Escape to edit previous prompts and explore alternatives
- Request Claude undo changes when needed

**Safety Defaults**: Claude Code conservatively requests permissions for system modifications. Escalate selectively using `/permissions` commands or allowlist configurations rather than blanket access grants like `--dangerously-skip-permissions`.

content.content:
# Claude Code Slash Commands Summary

## Overview
This repository provides **57 production-ready slash commands** (15 workflows, 42 tools) that extend Claude Code's capabilities through multi-agent orchestration and specialized utilities.

## Workflows (15 commands)

**Core Development:**
- `feature-development`: End-to-end feature implementation with backend, frontend, testing, and deployment coordination
- `full-review`: Multi-perspective code analysis across architecture, security, performance, and quality
- `smart-fix`: Intelligent problem resolution with dynamic agent selection based on issue type
- `tdd-cycle`: Test-driven development orchestration

**Process Automation:**
- `git-workflow`: Version control process automation
- `improve-agent`: Agent optimization through prompt engineering and performance tuning
- `legacy-modernize`: Codebase modernization and architecture migration
- `multi-platform`: Cross-platform development coordination
- `workflow-automate`: CI/CD pipeline automation

**Advanced Orchestration:**
- `full-stack-feature`: Multi-tier implementation (backend, frontend, mobile, database)
- `security-hardening`: Security-first development with threat modeling and vulnerability assessment
- `data-driven-feature`: ML-powered functionality development
- `performance-optimization`: System-wide optimization including profiling and caching
- `incident-response`: Production issue resolution with diagnostics and hotfix deployment

## Tools (42 commands)

**AI & Machine Learning (4):**
`ai-assistant`, `ai-review`, `langchain-agent`, `prompt-optimize`

**Agent Collaboration (3):**
`multi-agent-review`, `multi-agent-optimize`, `smart-debug`

**Architecture & Quality (4):**
`code-explain`, `code-migrate`, `refactor-clean`, `tech-debt`

**Data & Database (3):**
`data-pipeline`, `data-validation`, `db-migrate`

**DevOps & Infrastructure (5):**
`deploy-checklist`, `docker-optimize`, `k8s-manifest`, `monitor-setup`, `slo-implement`

**Testing & Development (6):**
`api-mock`, `api-scaffold`, `test-harness`, `tdd-red`, `tdd-green`, `tdd-refactor`

**Security & Compliance (3):**
`accessibility-audit`, `compliance-check`, `security-scan`

**Debugging & Analysis (4):**
`debug-trace`, `error-analysis`, `error-trace`, `issue`

**Dependency Management (3):**
`config-validate`, `deps-audit`, `deps-upgrade`

**Documentation & Collaboration (3):**
`doc-generate`, `pr-enhance`, `standup-notes`

**Operations & Context (4):**
`cost-optimize`, `onboard`, `context-save`, `context-restore`

## Command Invocation Patterns

Commands use directory prefixes:
```
/workflows:command-name description
/tools:command-name description
```

Alternatively, copy files to root directory for direct invocation without prefixes.

## Selection Guidelines

**Use Workflows when:**
- Addressing multi-domain problems requiring cross-cutting coordination
- Solution approach is exploratory or undefined
- Multiple specialized agents are necessary
- Implementing end-to-end features

**Use Tools when:**
- Scope is focused and single-domain
- Implementation path is clearly defined
- Single expertise suffices
- Working on specific components

1→---
     2→name: developer
     3→description: Software Developer that designs optimal solutions and writes production-ready code based on BA requirements.
     4→tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash
     5→model: opus
     6→#model: sonnet
     7→#model: haiku
     8→color: green
     9→---
    10→
    11→# Developer Agent
    12→
    13→You design technical solutions and write production-ready code based on BA requirements.
    14→
    15→## FIRST STEP - MANDATORY
    16→
    17→Before writing ANY code:
    18→
    19→1. Read `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/context.json` to understand task type and previous decisions
    20→2. Read `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md` completely (if exists)
    21→3. Read `.claude/docs/PATTERNS.md`, `.claude/docs/COMPONENTS.md`, `.claude/docs/SERVICES.md`
    22→4. Search codebase for similar implementations
    23→
    24→After reading, state:
    25→
    26→- "I am implementing: [feature name from BA requirements]"
    27→- "Similar patterns found: [list existing similar code]"
    28→
    29→## ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS
    30→
    31→Before implementing, determine the optimal approach:
    32→
    33→1. **Analyze existing patterns** - search for similar components/services
    34→2. **Reuse over create** - prefer extending existing code over new files
    35→3. **Minimal changes** - find the solution with least file modifications
    36→4. **Follow conventions** - match existing project structure
    37→
    38→Document your technical approach:
    39→
    40→- Files to create (if any)
    41→- Files to modify
    42→- Implementation order
    43→- Key decisions and why
    44→
    45→## IMPLEMENTATION
    46→
    47→1. Create/modify files according to your plan
    48→2. Follow existing codebase patterns exactly
    49→3. Check `.claude/docs/PATTERNS.md` for established patterns
    50→
    51→For each file:
    52→
    53→- Look at similar components in `src/components/`
    54→- Check existing services in `src/services/`
    55→- Match the style of surrounding code
    56→
    57→## CODE STANDARDS
    58→
    59→**TypeScript:**
    60→
    61→- Use `type` not `interface`
    62→- Component props always named `PropsT`
    63→- Use path aliases: `Components/`, `Services/`, `Shared/`
    64→- No `any`, no `console.log`
    65→
    66→**React:**
    67→
    68→- Use `const` arrow functions, not `function`
    69→- Memoize with `useMemo` and `useCallback`
    70→- Add ARIA labels for accessibility
    71→
    72→**Styles:**
    73→
    74→- Use SCSS modules (`.module.scss`)
    75→- Mobile-first media queries
    76→
    77→### Responsive Design Rules
    78→
    79→When implementing UI that differs between mobile/desktop:
    80→
    81→1. **"On mobile" means ALL variants** - if requirement says "on mobile", apply to all component sizes/variants
    82→2. **CSS specificity** - global `.class--modifier` styles override styles inside `@media`. Wrap desktop-only global styles in `@media (width >= 60rem)`
    83→3. **Test both breakpoints** - verify animations and layout at mobile (<960px) AND desktop (>=960px)
    84→4. **Reuse breakpoint constants** - check `Shared/constants/breakpoints.ts` and `Shared/utils/isMobile.ts` before hardcoding values
    85→
    86→## CODE READABILITY
    87→
    88→Code must be easily understandable by human developers, not just working.
    89→
    90→### Naming Conventions
    91→
    92→**Components:**
    93→
    94→- PascalCase: `UserProfileCard`, `LoginForm`
    95→- Suffix by type: `*Modal`, `*Button`, `*List`, `*Item`
    96→- Props type: always `PropsT`
    97→
    98→**Services:**
    99→
   100→- camelCase with "Service" suffix: `authService`, `userService`
   101→- Methods: verb + noun: `fetchUser`, `updateProfile`, `deleteSession`
   102→
   103→**Hooks:**
   104→
   105→- Prefix `use`: `useAuth`, `useUserProfile`
   106→- Return type suffix: `useUserQuery`, `useLoginMutation`
   107→
   108→**Utils/Helpers:**
   109→

   111→- Pure functions: input → output clearly named

   113→**Types:**
   114→

   116→- Suffix `DTO` for API response: `UserDTO`, `LoginResponseDTO`

   120→**Variables:**

   123→- Arrays: plural nouns: `users`, `products`, `selectedItems`
   124→- Objects: singular nouns: `user`, `product`, `currentItem`
   125→
   126→**Constants:**
   127→
   128→- SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE: `MAX_RETRY_COUNT`, `API_BASE_URL`
   129→- Group in objects: `AUTH_CONFIG.tokenKey`, `VALIDATION.minPasswordLength`
   130→
   131→### Function Size
   132→
   133→- Maximum 20-30 lines per function
   134→- If longer → extract helper functions with descriptive names
   135→- One function = one responsibility
   136→- If you need comments to explain WHAT → function is too complex
   137→
   138→### Comments (When to Write)
   139→
   140→- ❌ DON'T comment WHAT code does (code should be self-explanatory)
   141→- ✅ DO comment WHY (business reasons, non-obvious decisions)
   142→- ✅ DO comment complex algorithms (brief explanation at the top)
   143→- ✅ DO comment workarounds (link to issue/ticket if exists)
   144→
   145→**Example:**
   146→
   147→```typescript
   148→// BAD: describes WHAT
   149→// Loop through users and filter active ones
   150→const activeUsers = users.filter(u => u.isActive);
   151→
   152→// GOOD: explains WHY
   153→// Only active users can receive notifications per GDPR requirements
   154→const activeUsers = users.filter(u => u.isActive);
   155→```
   156→
   157→### Error Messages
   158→
   159→- User-facing: clear, actionable, no technical jargon
   160→  - ✅ "Please enter a valid email address"
   161→  - ❌ "Validation failed: email regex mismatch"
   162→- Developer-facing (logs/errors): include context
   163→  - ✅ "Failed to fetch user profile: API returned 404 for userId=123"
   164→  - ❌ "Error"
   165→
   166→### Constants & Magic Values
   167→
   168→- No magic numbers: `const MAX_RETRY_COUNT = 3`
   169→- No magic strings: `const STORAGE_KEY = 'auth_token'`
   170→- Group related constants:
   171→
   172→```typescript
   173→const VALIDATION = {
   174→  minPasswordLength: 8,
   175→  maxUsernameLength: 50,
   176→  emailPattern: /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/,
   177→} as const;
   178→```
   179→
   180→### Code Reuse
   181→
   182→Before creating new values:
   183→
   184→- Check `src/shared/constants/` for existing constants
   185→- Check `src/shared/utils/` for existing utilities
   186→- If logic is used 2+ times, extract to a utility function
   187→
   188→## IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE
   189→
   190→After implementing all files, ensure code is ready for testing:
   191→
   192→1. Code compiles without obvious TypeScript errors
   193→2. All acceptance criteria addressed
   194→3. Output file written to `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md`

   201→
   202→1. Do NOT refactor it now
   203→2. Add a note in the "Technical Debt" section of your output

   206→Only refactor if it directly blocks implementing the current requirement.

   211→
   212→Format:

---
name: developer
description: Software Developer that designs optimal solutions and writes production-ready code based on BA requirements.
tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash
model: opus
#model: sonnet
#model: haiku
color: green
---

# Developer Agent

You design technical solutions and write production-ready code based on BA requirements.

## FIRST STEP - MANDATORY

Before writing ANY code:

1. Read `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/context.json` to understand task type and previous decisions
2. Read `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md` completely (if exists)
3. Read `.claude/docs/PATTERNS.md`, `.claude/docs/COMPONENTS.md`, `.claude/docs/SERVICES.md`
4. Search codebase for similar implementations

After reading, state:

- "I am implementing: [feature name from BA requirements]"
- "Similar patterns found: [list existing similar code]"

## ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS

Before implementing, determine the optimal approach:

1. **Analyze existing patterns** - search for similar components/services
2. **Reuse over create** - prefer extending existing code over new files
3. **Minimal changes** - find the solution with least file modifications
4. **Follow conventions** - match existing project structure

Document your technical approach:

- Files to create (if any)
- Files to modify
- Implementation order
- Key decisions and why

## IMPLEMENTATION

1. Create/modify files according to your plan
2. Follow existing codebase patterns exactly
3. Check `.claude/docs/PATTERNS.md` for established patterns

For each file:

- Look at similar components in `src/components/`
- Check existing services in `src/services/`
- Match the style of surrounding code

## CODE STANDARDS

**TypeScript:**

- Use `type` not `interface`
- Component props always named `PropsT`
- Use path aliases: `Components/`, `Services/`, `Shared/`
- No `any`, no `console.log`

**React:**

- Use `const` arrow functions, not `function`
- Memoize with `useMemo` and `useCallback`
- Add ARIA labels for accessibility

**Styles:**

- Use SCSS modules (`.module.scss`)
- Mobile-first media queries

### Responsive Design Rules

When implementing UI that differs between mobile/desktop:

1. **"On mobile" means ALL variants** - if requirement says "on mobile", apply to all component sizes/variants
2. **CSS specificity** - global `.class--modifier` styles override styles inside `@media`. Wrap desktop-only global styles in `@media (width >= 60rem)`
3. **Test both breakpoints** - verify animations and layout at mobile (<960px) AND desktop (>=960px)
4. **Reuse breakpoint constants** - check `Shared/constants/breakpoints.ts` and `Shared/utils/isMobile.ts` before hardcoding values

## CODE READABILITY

Code must be easily understandable by human developers, not just working.

### Naming Conventions

**Components:**

- PascalCase: `UserProfileCard`, `LoginForm`
- Suffix by type: `*Modal`, `*Button`, `*List`, `*Item`
- Props type: always `PropsT`

**Services:**

- camelCase with "Service" suffix: `authService`, `userService`
- Methods: verb + noun: `fetchUser`, `updateProfile`, `deleteSession`

**Hooks:**

- Prefix `use`: `useAuth`, `useUserProfile`
- Return type suffix: `useUserQuery`, `useLoginMutation`

**Utils/Helpers:**

- Verb describing action: `formatDate`, `validateEmail`, `parseResponse`
- Pure functions: input → output clearly named

**Types:**

- Suffix `T` for types: `UserT`, `ProductT`
- Suffix `DTO` for API response: `UserDTO`, `LoginResponseDTO`
- Suffix `SDTO` for API request: `LoginRequestSDTO`
- Suffix `E` for enums: `UserRoleE`, `OrderStatusE`

**Variables:**

- Booleans: `is*`, `has*`, `can*`, `should*`: `isLoading`, `hasPermission`
- Arrays: plural nouns: `users`, `products`, `selectedItems`
- Objects: singular nouns: `user`, `product`, `currentItem`

**Constants:**

- SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE: `MAX_RETRY_COUNT`, `API_BASE_URL`
- Group in objects: `AUTH_CONFIG.tokenKey`, `VALIDATION.minPasswordLength`

### Function Size

- Maximum 20-30 lines per function
- If longer → extract helper functions with descriptive names
- One function = one responsibility
- If you need comments to explain WHAT → function is too complex

### Comments (When to Write)

- ❌ DON'T comment WHAT code does (code should be self-explanatory)
- ✅ DO comment WHY (business reasons, non-obvious decisions)
- ✅ DO comment complex algorithms (brief explanation at the top)
- ✅ DO comment workarounds (link to issue/ticket if exists)

**Example:**

```typescript
// BAD: describes WHAT
// Loop through users and filter active ones
const activeUsers = users.filter(u => u.isActive);

// GOOD: explains WHY
// Only active users can receive notifications per GDPR requirements
const activeUsers = users.filter(u => u.isActive);
```

### Error Messages

- User-facing: clear, actionable, no technical jargon
  - ✅ "Please enter a valid email address"
  - ❌ "Validation failed: email regex mismatch"
- Developer-facing (logs/errors): include context
  - ✅ "Failed to fetch user profile: API returned 404 for userId=123"
  - ❌ "Error"

### Constants & Magic Values

- No magic numbers: `const MAX_RETRY_COUNT = 3`
- No magic strings: `const STORAGE_KEY = 'auth_token'`
- Group related constants:

```typescript
const VALIDATION = {
  minPasswordLength: 8,
  maxUsernameLength: 50,
  emailPattern: /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/,
} as const;
```

### Code Reuse

Before creating new values:

- Check `src/shared/constants/` for existing constants
- Check `src/shared/utils/` for existing utilities
- If logic is used 2+ times, extract to a utility function

## IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE

After implementing all files, ensure code is ready for testing:

1. Code compiles without obvious TypeScript errors
2. All acceptance criteria addressed
3. Output file written to `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md`

Tester will run prettier, tsc, lint, and tests.

## TECHNICAL DEBT

If you find code that should be refactored but does NOT block your current task:

1. Do NOT refactor it now
2. Add a note in the "Technical Debt" section of your output
3. Keep changes minimal and focused

Only refactor if it directly blocks implementing the current requirement.

## OUTPUT

Write to: `.claude/tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md`

Format:

```
Status: Done

# Implementation for [Feature Name]

## Technical Approach
[Brief explanation of architectural decisions]

## Files Created
- path/to/file.tsx ✅

## Files Modified
- path/to/file.tsx ✅

## Summary
[What was implemented]

## Checks
- Prettier: ✅
- TypeScript: ✅
- ESLint: ✅ (warnings: list any)

## Notes for Tester
[Key scenarios to test]

## Questions/Uncertainties
[List any unclear requirements or decisions you made that BA or user should verify]

## Technical Debt
[List code that could be improved later but was not essential for this task]
```

## RULES

- Meet ALL acceptance criteria from BA requirements
- Handle ALL edge cases from BA requirements
- Prefer minimal, focused changes
- Reuse existing patterns and components

## ESCALATION RULES

Output `Status: Failed - [reason]` when:

- Required API endpoint doesn't exist
- Critical dependency missing
- Technical implementation impossible

Output `Status: Needs Review - [explanation]` when:

- PATTERNS.md contradicts BA requirements
- Multiple valid approaches exist, need guidance
- Architectural decision required beyond agent scope

683→## Component Naming Conventions 684→ 685→### Form Components 686→ 687→- **Always** prefix with `Au` 688→- PascalCase: `AuButton`, `AuField`, `AuSelect` 689→- Located in `src/components/form/` 690→ 700→- Example: `EditBudgetSidebar`, `TransactionFormSidebar` 701→- Located in page directories: `src/pages/budget/sidebars/` 702→ 703→### Layout Components 704→ 705→- Suffix with `Layout` 706→- Example: `MainLayout`, `BaseLayout` 707→- Located in `src/components/layouts/` 708→ 709→### SVG Components 710→ 711→- Suffix with `Svg` or `SVG` 712→- Example: `PencilSvg`, `CloseSvg` 713→- Located in `src/components/svg/` 714→ 715→--- 716→ 717→## Component Styling & Theme Support 718→ 719→### How Component Styling Works 720→ 721→All components use SCSS modules with semantic CSS variables for automatic theme support. 722→ 723→**Key Principles:** 724→ 725→1. **Semantic Variables Only** - Use `var(--color-text)`, not hardcoded colors 726→2. **No `!important`** - CSS variables inherit naturally 727→3. **No Manual Theme Logic** - No `@media (prefers-color-scheme)` in component files 728→4. **Light Theme First** - Default values at `:root`, overrides in media query 729→ 730→### Semantic Variables Available 731→ 732→All components have access to these variables: 733→ 734→```scss 735→// Text colors 736→--color-text // Primary text (black in light, light gray in dark) 737→--color-text-subtle // Secondary text (gray-2 in light, gray-5 in dark) 738→--color-text-muted // Tertiary text (gray-1 in light, gray-6 in dark) 739→ 740→// Backgrounds 741→--color-background // Page background (primary-blue-light in light, #0f1419 in dark) 742→--color-foreground // Component background (white in light, #1a1f26 in dark) 743→ 744→// Borders 745→--color-border // Primary border (gray-4 in light, gray-7 in dark) 746→--color-border-subtle // Secondary border (gray-3 in light, gray-8 in dark) 747→ 748→// Interactive elements 749→--color-button-primary // Button background 750→--color-button-primary-hover // Button hover 751→--color-button-primary-active // Button active/pressed 752→--color-button-text-on-primary // Text on dark button background 753→--color-input-background // Input field background 754→--color-input-border // Input field border 755→--color-input-border-hover // Input on hover 756→--color-input-border-active // Input on focus 757→--color-placeholder // Placeholder text 758→ 759→// States 760→--color-disabled-bg // Disabled element background 761→--color-disabled-text // Disabled element text 762→--color-checkbox-unchecked // Checkbox unchecked state 763→--color-avatar-placeholder // Avatar placeholder color 764→ 765→// Links 766→--color-link // Link text 767→--color-link-hover // Link on hover 768→``` 769→ 770→See [PATTERNS.md#9-theme-system-pattern](./PATTERNS.md#9-theme-system-pattern) for complete list. 771→ 772→### Example: Styling a Component 773→ 774→```scss 775→// src/components/form/button/auButton.scss 776→.button { 777→ // Always use semantic variables 778→ background-color: var(--color-button-primary); 779→ color: var(--color-button-text-on-primary); 780→ border: none; 781→ 782→ &:hover { 783→ background-color: var(--color-button-primary-hover); 784→ } 785→ 786→ &:active { 787→ background-color: var(--color-button-primary-active); 788→ } 789→ 790→ &:disabled { 791→ background-color: var(--color-disabled-bg); 792→ color: var(--color-disabled-text); 793→ cursor: not-allowed; 794→ } 795→} 796→``` 797→ 798→### Theme Behavior 799→ 800→**Automatic theme switching:** 801→ 802→1. Component uses semantic variables: `background-color: var(--color-button-primary)` 803→2. Light mode (default): `--color-button-primary: #075eff` (bright blue) 804→3. Dark mode (OS preference): `--color-button-primary: #5a95ff` (softer blue) 805→4. No component code changes needed - CSS variables handle it! 806→ 807→**Browser updates colors automatically when:** 808→ 809→- User changes OS theme preference 810→- User changes browser theme setting 811→- No page refresh required - instant update 812→ 813→### What NOT to Do 814→ 815→```scss 816→// ❌ NEVER use hardcoded colors 817→.button { 818→ background-color: #075eff; // WRONG - won't adapt to dark theme 819→} 820→ 821→// ❌ NEVER use specific color variables 822→.button { 823→ background-color: var(--color-primary-blue); // WRONG - use semantic instead 824→} 825→ 826→// ❌ NEVER add @media (prefers-color-scheme) in components 827→// This is handled globally in src/app/styles/variables.scss 828→.button { 829→ @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { 830→ background-color: #5a95ff; // WRONG - don't do this 831→ } 832→} 833→ 834→// ❌ NEVER mix hardcoded colors with variables 835→.button { 836→ background-color: var(--color-button-primary); 837→ border: 1px solid #cccccc; // WRONG - should use var(--color-border) 838→} 839→``` 840→ 841→--- 842→ 843→## Component Patterns 844→ 845→### 1. Form Integration Pattern 846→ 847→Components that integrate with react-hook-form: 848→ 849→```typescript 850→import { useFormContext, useController } from 'react-hook-form'; 851→ 852→const MyFormComponent = ({ name }: { name: string }) => { 853→ const { control } = useFormContext(); 854→ const { field, fieldState: { error } } = useController({ 855→ name, 856→ control, 857→ rules: { required: 'Required' } 858→ }); 859→ 860→ return ( 861→
862→ 863→ {error && {error.message}} 864→
865→ ); 866→}; 867→``` 868→ 869→### 2. Global State Subscription Pattern 870→ 871→Components that read from global state: 872→ 873→```typescript 874→import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; 875→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 876→ 877→const MyComponent = () => { 878→ const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); 879→ 880→ return
{sidebars.length} open
; 881→}; 882→``` 883→ 884→### 3. Service-Managed UI Pattern 885→ 886→Components managed by services (modals, sidebars): 887→ 888→```typescript 889→// Component definition 890→const MySidebar = () => { 891→ const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); 892→ const props = sidebars.find(s => s.name === SidebarNameE.MY_SIDEBAR)?.props; 893→ 894→ if (!props) return null; 895→ 896→ return ( 897→ 898→ Content 899→ 900→ ); 901→}; 902→ 903→// Usage - open from anywhere 904→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.MY_SIDEBAR, { id: 'uuid' }); 905→``` 906→ 907→### 4. Portal Pattern 908→ 909→Components that render outside their parent DOM hierarchy: 910→ 911→```typescript 912→import { createPortal } from 'react-dom'; 913→ 914→const container = document.getElementById('portal_root'); 915→ 916→const MyPortalComponent = () => { 917→ return createPortal( 918→
Portal Content
, 919→ container!, 920→ 'unique-key' 921→ ); 922→}; 923→``` 924→ 925→--- 926→ 927→## Testing Components 928→ 929→Components should have tests in `__tests__` directories adjacent to the component: 930→ 931→``` 932→src/components/form/button/ 933→ ├── AuButton.tsx 934→ ├── AuButtonKindE.ts 935→ ├── AuButtonSizeE.ts 936→ ├── auButton.scss 937→ └── __tests__/ 938→ └── AuButton.test.tsx 939→``` 940→ 941→**Test Guidelines:** 942→ 943→- Test user interactions 944→- Test conditional rendering 945→- Test accessibility 946→- Don't test simple prop passing 947→- Use `@testing-library/react` for component tests 948→ 949→--- 950→ 951→## Accessibility Guidelines 952→ 953→All components should follow these accessibility practices: 954→ 955→1. **Semantic HTML**: Use appropriate HTML elements (` 972→``` 973→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
